label: demo: fix failing auth test
detail: Hand-written sample session demonstrating branching, failure, backtrack and convergence.

label: Fix the failing test in the auth module
detail: Fix the failing test in the auth module. `pytest tests/test_auth.py` fails on test_token_refresh. Don't change the test itself.

label: Run the test first to see the actual failure
detail: I should reproduce the failure before reading any code. Running the single failing test with -x to get the traceback.

label: Bash: pytest tests/test_auth.py::test_token_refresh -x
detail: $ pytest tests/test_auth.py::test_token_refresh -x

label: FAILED — AssertionError: token not refreshed
detail:
E  AssertionError: assert new_token != old_token
E  refresh_token() returned the same token when expiry was within skew window
tests/test_auth.py:88: AssertionError

label: Token not rotating near expiry — inspect refresh logic + config in parallel
detail: The refresh path returns a cached token when expiry is within the skew window. Need to see refresh_token() and the skew config. Reading both files in parallel.

label: Read auth/refresh.py
detail: Read auth/refresh.py (214 lines)

label: Read auth/config.py
detail: Read auth/config.py (61 lines)

label: Suspect: cache check happens before skew check
detail: refresh_token() consults the in-memory cache before evaluating expiry skew. Two candidate fixes: (a) reorder the checks, (b) invalidate cache entry when within skew. Trying (a) first — smaller diff.

label: Edit auth/refresh.py — reorder cache/skew checks
detail: Moved `if within_skew(token):` above the cache lookup so near-expiry tokens bypass the cache.

label: Bash: pytest tests/test_auth.py -x
detail: $ pytest tests/test_auth.py -x

label: FAILED — 3 other tests now break (cache never hit)
detail:
FAILED tests/test_auth.py::test_cached_token_reused
FAILED tests/test_auth.py::test_no_network_when_fresh
FAILED tests/test_auth.py::test_rate_limit_respected
Reordering made every call skip the cache when skew=0 default.

label: Reordering breaks caching — revert, try invalidation instead
detail: Approach (a) is wrong: the reorder bypasses the cache for all tokens because within_skew() treats skew=0 as always-true. Reverting and switching to approach (b): keep check order, but explicitly invalidate the cache entry when the token is inside the skew window.

label: Edit auth/refresh.py — revert reorder
detail: Restored original check order in refresh_token().

label: Approach (b): invalidate cache inside skew window
detail: Keep the cache lookup first, but before returning a cached token verify it is not within the skew window; if it is, drop it from the cache and fall through to a real refresh. Also fix within_skew() to return False when skew=0.

label: Read auth/cache.py
detail: Read auth/cache.py (90 lines) — confirm invalidate() exists and is threadsafe.

label: Edit auth/refresh.py — invalidate near-expiry cache entries
Edit auth/refresh.py — within_skew(skew=0) returns False
detail: Guard clause: `if skew <= 0: return False` so default config never treats fresh tokens as expiring.

label: Bash: pytest tests/test_auth.py
detail: $ pytest tests/test_auth.py

label: Converged: cache invalidation fix, full suite green
detail: Settled on approach (b) after (a) broke cache-dependent tests. Running the wider suite once to be safe.

label: Bash: pytest tests/ -q
detail: $ pytest tests/ -q

label: 148 passed, 2 skipped
detail: 148 passed, 2 skipped in 21.8s

label: Fixed: near-expiry tokens were served from cache
detail: The bug: refresh_token() returned cached tokens even when they were inside the expiry skew window. Fix: invalidate the cache entry when a cached token is within skew, plus a guard so skew=0 never matches. First attempt (reordering the checks) was reverted because it bypassed the cache entirely. Full suite: 148 passed.
